items.title: Tabular classification baseline
items.description: Banks, insurers, and SaaS churn models often start with a dense MLP over engineered numeric and one-hot features — hidden layers capture nonlinear risk combinations before a sigmoid or softmax head.

items.title: MNIST and small-image baselines
items.description: Flattened-pixel MLPs (784 → hidden → 10) remain a teaching and benchmarking baseline for digit classification before introducing convolutional inductive bias.

items.title: Embedding-to-label classifier
items.description: Precomputed text or user embeddings (e.g. 768-d) feed a shallow MLP head for sentiment, topic, or fraud labels — the hidden stack adapts generic embeddings to the downstream task.

items.title: Transformer feed-forward sublayer
items.description: Each Transformer block contains an MLP (two linear layers with GELU/ReLU) applied per token — the same hidden-layer pattern at scale inside modern language and vision models.
